task.title:
[Context] GitHub issue #25: POST /api/generate. Architecture §4.2 and api_contract.yaml define the endpoint. Several pieces already exist and must be reused, not rewritten:
- ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/conditioner.py → build_system_prompt(style_profile, rag_chunks)
- ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/fit_scorer.py → compute_fit_score(text, style_profile, nlp, embedding_model)
- backend/app/services/watsonx_client.py → generate(prompt, system_prompt, model_id, params) (sync, 8s timeout + retries); raise WatsonxError
- ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/db.py → retrieve_top_k(query_embedding, k=5)
- ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/passport/builder.py → issue_passport(...) → {jws_token, json_payload}
- backend/app/routes/authors.py shows the slug→UUID + latest StyleProfile pattern (lines ~262–291)

Note: ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/generator.py does NOT exist. Watsonx lives in watsonx_client.py. Prefer a thin orchestrator (either new generator.py OR logic inside routes/generate.py) — propose which and why.

[Task] Before writing any code, produce an implementation plan for issue #25 end-to-end.

[Restriction]
- Match docs/api_contract.yaml exactly (GenerateRequest / GenerationOutput / GenerateResponse / PassportEnvelope).
- Same model + same params for vanilla and AutorIA; only system_prompt differs (vanilla = None).
- Parallel Watsonx via asyncio.gather + asyncio.to_thread (client is sync).
- Do NOT invent a new fit_score formula — call compute_fit_score.
- Do NOT put raw user prompt in the passport (issue_passport already hashes).
- 404 if author slug missing OR StyleProfile missing (same error shape as authors.py).
- 503 on WatsonxError / timeout.
- Target P95 < 8s (MVP §4.3).

[Output]
1. Ordered list of files to create/modify
2. Sequence diagram of the handler in 8–12 steps
3. Risks (empty embeddings / no RAG, stub StyleProfile zeros, model load latency for spaCy/ST)
4. Proposed model_id constant and where it lives
5. Test strategy (unit + mocked e2e)
Do NOT write code yet.

data.content:
<role_definition>
You are GenerationConductor, an LLM-orchestration engineer specialized in retrieval-augmented prompting and style-conditioned generation for AutorIA. You help P3 build the pipeline that turns a user prompt into a vanilla Watsonx response and a StyleProfile-conditioned response in parallel, scores both with fit_score, and mints the Authorship Passport for the conditioned one. You think empirically: every prompt change is paired with an A/B fit_score measurement on a fixed prompt suite, and you are paranoid about P95 latency (target under 8s).
</role_definition>

<investigate_before_answering>
Never speculate about code you have not opened. If the user references a specific file, you MUST read the file before answering. Investigate and read relevant files before answering questions about the codebase. Never make any claims about code before investigating unless you are certain of the correct answer — give grounded and hallucination-free answers.
</investigate_before_answering>

<engineering_discipline>
Produce the minimal change that solves the problem. Do not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what was asked. Do not add error handling for scenarios that cannot happen. Do not clean up surrounding code that is unrelated to the task. If you write something and a simpler version would work, use the simpler version. Every changed line should trace directly to the user's request.
</engineering_discipline>

<tool_use>
- Choose the most appropriate tool for each step.
- Tools can be called in parallel when independent, but avoid running operations together that have dependencies (e.g., don't run edits and commands that depend on those edits in the same batch).
- If a tool fails, address the error before continuing.
- MCP server tools use IDs formatted as `mcp__<server>__<tool>`. Treat these as Model Context Protocol tools; the middle segment identifies the owning MCP server. Both segments may be truncated if the full name exceeds the 64-character API limit. Built-in and source tools use their normal IDs and are categorized by their descriptions and capability groups.
- Prefer native file tools (read_file, write_file, search_and_replace, insert_content, list_files) over shell commands for file operations — they need no additional user permissions. Use shell/execute tools only for operations that genuinely require them: builds, test runners, git commands, and system operations.
</tool_use>

<markdown_rules>
All responses should show any language construct or filename reference as a clickable link, exactly as [`filename OR language.declaration()`](relative/file/path.ext:line); line is required for syntax references and optional for filename links.
</markdown_rules>

<auto_appended_context>
Each user message may contain an auto-appended <environment_details> section that includes potentially relevant information such as git status, active file, and relevant files modified externally since your last interaction. This section is automatically appended by the system to provide additional context that may possibly help you and is not part of the user's query.

Pay close attention to the <external_changes> sub-section — it lists files that were modified, deleted, or created outside of your actions. Before writing or editing any file, check whether it appears in <external_changes> and incorporate those changes to avoid conflicts.
</auto_appended_context>

<base_rules>
- Prefer editing tools (apply_diff, search_and_replace, insert_content) over write_file for existing files.
- When using write_file, provide the COMPLETE file content. Partial updates or placeholders are not acceptable.
- Be direct and technical. Do not start messages with "Great", "Certainly", "Okay", or "Sure".
- Follow existing code style and project conventions.
- Use available tools to accomplish tasks efficiently. Do not ask unnecessary follow-up questions.
- Do not stop when implementation looks complete. Finish the task only when all requested requirements are met and the resulting state is positive.
- Before reporting final completion, run the relevant validation for your changes. Use the checks that apply to the work you changed, such as lint, tests, typecheck, formatting, builds, or other project validation.
- Work is done when the changed work and any affected project state pass the required validation with no new warnings, failures, or unfinished requirements.
</base_rules>

You have access to skills that provide specialized instructions for specific tasks. Review the available skills below. When a user's request matches a skill's description, activate it using the use_skill tool to load detailed instructions before proceeding. Only activate skills that are clearly relevant to the current task. Activate each skill once per current context window. If context was compacted and you need the instructions again, you may re-activate that skill. Only call use_skill for skills listed here — do not attempt to invoke skills not present in this list.
<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>create-skill</name>
    <description>Guide for creating a new Bob skill (SKILL.md) — covers the frontmatter schema, name rules, file location, and gotchas. Use when the user wants to author, create, or scaffold a skill.</description>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>create-mode</name>
    <description>Guide for creating a new custom Bob mode (custom_modes.yaml) — covers the schema, permission groups, roleDefinition, and file location. Use when the user wants to author, create, or scaffold a mode or persona.</description>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>configure-mcp</name>
    <description>Add a new MCP server or diagnose an existing one — covers local vs remote transport, system dependency checks (node, docker, podman, python, uv), config schema validation, secrets handling, and scope. Use when the user wants to add, register, configure, or troubleshoot an MCP server.</description>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>build-mcp-server</name>
    <description>Guide for building a custom MCP server from scratch — covers project scaffolding, the v2 registerTool/registerResource/registerPrompt API, API key and OAuth authentication patterns, building and registering the server. Use when the user wants to create, build, or write a custom MCP server.</description>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>xlsx-insights</name>
    <description>Use this skill when the user asks for analysis, extraction, or insights from an Excel (.xlsx) workbook. It samples the file, generates a small Node script to compute the answer, runs it, and summarises the result.</description>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

<user_custom_instructions>
The following additional instructions are provided by the user, and should be followed to the best of your ability without interfering with the TOOL USE guidelines.

Language Preference:
You should always speak and think in the "English" (en) language unless the user gives you instructions below to do otherwise.

Mode-specific Instructions:
Do not inflate the system prompt: every token costs latency; if an instruction does not move fit_score, remove it. Never change the LLM call signature so that vanilla vs AutorIA stays an honest comparison (same model, same params, only the system prompt differs). Evaluate on a fixed suite of at least 5 prompts, never a single point. Never leak the raw user prompt into the Passport: store user_prompt_hash only.
</user_custom_instructions>

<environment_info>

The currently active workspace folder is c:\Users\pablo\Desktop\UPV\3º INF\IBM\autorIA
Use file paths relative to this directory.
## Command Execution Environment
Platform: win32
Release: 10.0.26200
Architecture: x64
Shell: powershell.exe
</environment_info>

You can get information about available symbols by using the `GetSymbolsOverview` tool for finding top-level symbols in a file,
or by using `FindSymbol` if you already know the symbol's name path. You generally try to read as little code as possible
while still solving your task, meaning you only read the bodies when you need to, and after you have found the symbol you want to edit.
For example, if you are working with python code and already know that you need to read the body of the constructor of the class Foo, you can directly
use `FindSymbol` with the name path `Foo/__init__` and `include_body: true`. If you don't know yet which methods in `Foo` you need to read or edit,
you can use `FindSymbol` with the name path `Foo`, `include_body: false` and `depth: 1` to get all (top-level) methods of `Foo` before proceeding
to read the desired methods with `include_body: true`
You can understand relationships between symbols by using the `FindReferencingSymbols` tool.

# Mode Switching

You can switch between different modes using the switch_mode tool. Each mode provides access to different capabilities and tools optimized for specific types of tasks.

    <available_tools>GetSymbolsOverview, FindSymbol, FindReferencingSymbols, use_skill, apply_diff, create_html_artifact, insert_content, list_files, read_file, read_xlsx, search_and_replace, update_todo_list, switch_mode, write_file, execute_command, glob, grep, spawn_subagent, ask_followup_question, start_subtask, start_workflow</available_tools>
  </mode>
  <mode>
    <name>Plan</name>
    <id>plan</id>
    <when_to_use>Use this mode when you need to plan, design, or strategize before implementation. Perfect for breaking down complex problems, creating technical specifications, designing system architecture, or brainstorming solutions before coding.</when_to_use>
    <available_tools>GetSymbolsOverview, FindSymbol, FindReferencingSymbols, use_skill, apply_diff, insert_content, list_files, read_file, read_xlsx, search_and_replace, switch_mode, write_file, glob, grep, spawn_subagent, ask_followup_question, start_workflow</available_tools>
  </mode>

    <when_to_use>Use this mode when you need explanations, documentation, or answers to technical questions. Best for understanding concepts, analyzing existing code, getting recommendations, or learning about technologies without making changes.</when_to_use>
    <available_tools>GetSymbolsOverview, FindSymbol, FindReferencingSymbols, use_skill, list_files, read_file, read_xlsx, switch_mode, glob, grep, spawn_subagent, ask_followup_question, start_workflow</available_tools>
  </mode>
  <mode>
    <name>StyleExtractor</name>
    <id>style-extractor</id>
    <when_to_use>Building or debugging the linguistic feature extractor (lexical, syntactic, stylistic, vocabulary) and the StyleProfile schema.</when_to_use>
    <available_tools>GetSymbolsOverview, FindSymbol, FindReferencingSymbols, apply_diff, insert_content, list_files, read_file, read_xlsx, search_and_replace, switch_mode, write_file, execute_command, glob, grep, ask_followup_question, start_workflow</available_tools>
  </mode>
  <mode>
    <name>GenerationConductor</name>
    <id>generation-conductor</id>
    <when_to_use>RAG retrieval, conditioned-prompt design, Watsonx orchestration, and fit_score work.</when_to_use>
    <available_tools>GetSymbolsOverview, FindSymbol, FindReferencingSymbols, apply_diff, insert_content, list_files, read_file, read_xlsx, search_and_replace, switch_mode, write_file, execute_command, glob, grep, ask_followup_question, start_workflow</available_tools>
  </mode>

    <when_to_use>Style DNA charts, side-by-side generation UI, /verify layout, API client and TS types, and i18n.</when_to_use>

# glob — file search
Use `glob` to find files by name pattern (e.g. "**/*.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts"). Prefer this over `list_files` when looking for specific files across the project.
Only fall back to `list_files` when you need to browse a single directory's structure.

# grep — content search
Use `grep` to find code by content using regex patterns. Prefer this over `list_files` + `read_file` when searching for specific code, symbols, or patterns.
Use `read_file` only after grep tells you which file and lines to look at.

data.content: I'll investigate all the referenced files before producing the plan.

Contents of file ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/conditioner.py:

1 | """Conditioned system-prompt builder.
2 | 
3 | Public API
4 | ----------
5 | build_system_prompt(style_profile, rag_chunks) -> str
6 |     Composes the Watsonx (Llama 3.3 70B) system prompt that conditions the LLM
7 |     to adopt an author's exact style, following the template in docs/MVP.md §4.3.
8 | 
9 | Latency contract
10 | ----------------
11 | The returned string is kept under ~1200 tokens (architecture.md §6).  The
12 | safeguard is applied by truncating ``rag_chunks`` to at most 5 items before
13 | they are interpolated.
14 | """
15 | 
16 | from __future__ import annotations
17 | 
18 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19 | # Constants
20 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21 | 
22 | _MAX_CHUNKS: int = 5
23 | _MAX_VOCAB_TERMS: int = 15
24 | 
25 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26 | # Public function
27 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28 | 
29 | _TEMPLATE = (
30 |     "Write in the style of author {author_id}. "
31 |     "Your writing must have: average sentence length ~{avg_sentence_length} tokens "
32 |     "with high variation, {subordination_rule}, and vocabulary including terms like "
33 |     "{vocab_list}. "
34 |     "Here are example passages: {chunks}. "
35 |     "Write only in that style; do not explain."
36 | )
37 | 
38 | 
39 | def build_system_prompt(style_profile: dict, rag_chunks: list[str]) -> str:
40 |     """Compose the conditioned system prompt for the Watsonx LLM.
41 | 
42 |     Parameters
43 |     ----------
44 |     style_profile:
45 |         A StyleProfile v1.0 dict (see ``autoria_ai/schemas/style_profile.json``).
46 |         Missing keys are handled with safe fallbacks so the function never raises
47 |         on a partial profile.
48 |     rag_chunks:
49 |         Retrieved example passages (top-k by cosine similarity from pgvector).
50 |         At most ``_MAX_CHUNKS`` (5) are used; any extras are silently dropped to
51 |         keep the prompt within the ~1200-token latency budget.
52 | 
53 |     Returns
54 |     -------
55 |     str
56 |         A fully-rendered system prompt string ready to be passed as the
57 |         ``system`` parameter of a Watsonx chat-completion call.
58 |     """
59 |     # -- author id -------------------------------------------------------------
60 |     author_id: str = style_profile.get("author_id", "unknown")
61 | 
62 |     # -- avg sentence length ---------------------------------------------------
63 |     syntactic: dict = style_profile.get("syntactic", {})
64 |     avg_sentence_length: float = syntactic.get("avg_sentence_length_tokens", 20.0)
65 |     # Format as integer-like when it's a whole number, otherwise one decimal.
66 |     avg_sl_str = (
67 |         str(int(avg_sentence_length))
68 |         if avg_sentence_length == int(avg_sentence_length)
69 |         else f"{avg_sentence_length:.1f}"
70 |     )
71 | 
72 |     # -- subordination rule (natural language translation) ---------------------
73 |     subordination_ratio: float = syntactic.get("subordination_ratio", 0.0)
74 |     if subordination_ratio >= 0.3:
75 |         subordination_rule = "heavy use of subordinate clauses"
76 |     elif subordination_ratio >= 0.15:
77 |         subordination_rule = "moderate use of subordinate clauses"
78 |     else:
79 |         subordination_rule = "straightforward clause structure with few subordinate clauses"
80 | 
81 |     #  -- distinctive vocab (top 10-15 terms to avoid token bloat) -------------
82 |     raw_vocab: list[dict] = style_profile.get("distinctive_vocab", [])
83 |     top_terms: list[str] = [
84 |         entry["term"] for entry in raw_vocab[:_MAX_VOCAB_TERMS] if "term" in entry
85 |     ]
86 |     vocab_list: str = ", ".join(top_terms) if top_terms else "vivid and precise language"
87 | 
88 |     # -- rag chunks (enforce hard cap of 5) ------------------------------------
89 |     safe_chunks: list[str] = rag_chunks[:_MAX_CHUNKS]
90 |     chunks_text = " | ".join(safe_chunks) if safe_chunks else "(no example passages provided)"
91 | 
92 |     return _TEMPLATE.format(
93 |         author_id=author_id,
94 |         avg_sentence_length=avg_sl_str,
95 |         subordination_rule=subordination_rule,
96 |         vocab_list=vocab_list,
97 |         chunks=chunks_text,
98 |     )
99 |

Contents of file ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/fit_scorer.py:

1 | """fit_score — Composite authorial-fit metric.
2 | 
3 | Public API
4 | ----------
5 | compute_fit_score(generated_text, style_profile, nlp, embedding_model) -> int
6 |     Returns an integer in [0, 100] measuring how closely a generated text
7 |     matches a target author's StyleProfile v1.0.
8 | 
9 | Algorithm (docs/style_features.md §6)
10 | --------------------------------------
11 | fit_score = semx0.35 + synx0.20 + lexx0.15 + styx0.15 + vocx0.15
12 | 
13 | Each sub-score is clamped to [0, 1] before weighting.
14 | Final score is rounded and clamped to [0, 100] (int).
15 | """
16 | 
17 | from __future__ import annotations
18 | 
19 | from typing import Any
20 | 
21 | import numpy as np
22 | from scipy.spatial.distance import cosine as cosine_distance
23 | 
24 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25 | # Internal helpers
26 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27 | 
28 | _POS_TAGS = ("NOUN", "VERB", "ADJ", "ADV", "DET", "ADP", "PRON", "CONJ", "SCONJ", "OTHER")
29 | 
30 | 
31 | def _clamp01(value: float) -> float:
32 |     return max(0.0, min(1.0, value))
33 | 
34 | 
35 | def _semantic_score(
36 |     generated_text: str,
37 |     centroid: list[float],
38 |     embedding_model: Any,
39 | ) -> float:
40 |     """Cosine similarity between the generated text embedding and the author centroid."""
41 |     centroid_vec = np.array(centroid, dtype=np.float32)
42 |     generated_vec = np.array(embedding_model.encode(generated_text), dtype=np.float32).ravel()
43 |     # scipy cosine returns distance; convert to similarity.
44 |     # Guard against zero vectors (cosine_distance raises on zero norm).
45 |     if np.linalg.norm(generated_vec) == 0.0 or np.linalg.norm(centroid_vec) == 0.0:
46 |         return 0.0
47 |     return float(1.0 - cosine_distance(generated_vec, centroid_vec))
48 | 
49 | 
50 | def _syntactic_score(doc: Any, asl_profile: float) -> float:
51 |     """1 - |asl_generated - asl_profile| / asl_profile."""
52 |     if asl_profile == 0.0:
53 |         return 0.0
54 |     lengths = [len(sent) for sent in doc.sents]
55 |     if not lengths:
56 |         return 0.0
57 |     asl_generated = sum(lengths) / len(lengths)
58 |     return float(1.0 - abs(asl_generated - asl_profile) / asl_profile)
59 | 
60 | 
61 | def _lexical_score(doc: Any, mattr_profile: float) -> float:
62 |     """1 - |ttr_generated - mattr_profile| / mattr_profile.
63 | 
64 |     ttr_generated = unique_lemmas / total_lemmas  (punctuation excluded).
65 |     """
66 |     if mattr_profile == 0.0:
67 |         return 0.0
68 |     lemmas = [t.lemma_.lower() for t in doc if not t.is_punct]
69 |     if not lemmas:
70 |         return 0.0
71 |     ttr_generated = len(set(lemmas)) / len(lemmas)
72 |     return float(1.0 - abs(ttr_generated - mattr_profile) / mattr_profile)
73 | 
74 | 
75 | def _pos_distribution(doc: Any) -> dict[str, float]:
76 |     """Relative frequency of each tracked POS tag (excluding punctuation).
77 | 
78 |     Follows the same logic as docs/style_features.md §3.2.
79 |     """
80 |     tracked = {"NOUN", "VERB", "ADJ", "ADV", "DET", "ADP", "PRON", "CONJ", "SCONJ"}
81 |     counts: dict[str, int] = dict.fromkeys(_POS_TAGS, 0)
82 |     for token in doc:
83 |         if token.is_punct:
84 |             continue
85 |         if token.pos_ in tracked:
86 |             counts[token.pos_] += 1
87 |         else:
88 |             counts["OTHER"] += 1
89 | 
90 |     total_tracked = sum(counts[p] for p in tracked)
91 |     if total_tracked == 0:
92 |         return dict.fromkeys(_POS_TAGS, 0.0)
93 | 
94 |     dist: dict[str, float] = {p: counts[p] / total_tracked for p in tracked}
95 |     dist["OTHER"] = 1.0 - sum(dist.values())
96 |     return dist
97 | 
98 | 
99 | def _stylistic_score(doc: Any, pos_dist_profile: dict[str, float]) -> float:
100 |     """Jaccard similarity between generated and profile POS distributions.
101 | 
102 |     Jaccard(A, B) = Σ min(A[k], B[k]) / Σ max(A[k], B[k])
103 |     over the union of keys in both dicts.
104 |     """
105 |     pos_dist_gen = _pos_distribution(doc)
106 | 
107 |     all_keys = set(pos_dist_gen) | set(pos_dist_profile)
108 |     numerator = sum(min(pos_dist_gen.get(k, 0.0), pos_dist_profile.get(k, 0.0)) for k in all_keys)
109 |     denominator = sum(max(pos_dist_gen.get(k, 0.0), pos_dist_profile.get(k, 0.0)) for k in all_keys)
110 |     if denominator == 0.0:
111 |         return 0.0
112 |     return float(numerator / denominator)
113 | 
114 | 
115 | def _vocabulary_score(doc: Any, distinctive_vocab: list[dict]) -> float:
116 |     """len(generated_lemmas ∩ top30_distinctive) / 30."""
117 |     top30: set[str] = {entry["term"] for entry in distinctive_vocab[:30] if "term" in entry}
118 |     if not top30:
119 |         return 0.0
120 |     generated_lemmas = {t.lemma_.lower() for t in doc if t.is_alpha}
121 |     return float(len(generated_lemmas & top30) / 30)
122 | 
123 | 
124 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125 | # Public API
126 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127 | 
128 | 
129 | def compute_fit_score(
130 |     generated_text: str,
131 |     style_profile: dict,
132 |     nlp: Any,  # loaded spaCy model (en_core_web_lg)
133 |     embedding_model: Any,  # loaded sentence-transformers model
134 | ) -> int:
135 |     """Compute the composite fit_score for a generated text against a StyleProfile."""
136 |     # 1. Ejecutar spaCy UNA sola vez
137 |     doc = nlp(generated_text)
138 |     # -- semantic (0.35) -------------------------------------------------------
139 |     # Este usa sentence-transformers, así que le pasamos el texto en crudo
140 |     centroid: list[float] = style_profile.get("semantic_centroid", [])
141 |     sem = _clamp01(_semantic_score(generated_text, centroid, embedding_model))
142 | 
143 |     # -- syntactic (0.20) ------------------------------------------------------
144 |     syntactic: dict = style_profile.get("syntactic", {})
145 |     asl_profile: float = syntactic.get("avg_sentence_length_tokens", 0.0)
146 |     syn = _clamp01(_syntactic_score(doc, asl_profile))
147 | 
148 |     # -- lexical (0.15) --------------------------------------------------------
149 |     lexical: dict = style_profile.get("lexical", {})
150 |     mattr_profile: float = lexical.get("mattr_500", 0.0)
151 |     lex = _clamp01(_lexical_score(doc, mattr_profile))
152 | 
153 |     # -- stylistic (0.15) — POS Jaccard ----------------------------------------
154 |     stylistic: dict = style_profile.get("stylistic", {})
155 |     pos_dist_profile: dict[str, float] = stylistic.get("pos_distribution", {})
156 |     sty = _clamp01(_stylistic_score(doc, pos_dist_profile))
157 | 
158 |     # -- vocabulary (0.15) -----------------------------------------------------
159 |     distinctive_vocab: list[dict] = style_profile.get("distinctive_vocab", [])
160 |     voc = _clamp01(_vocabulary_score(doc, distinctive_vocab))
161 | 
162 |     # -- composite -------------------------------------------------------------
163 |     raw = sem * 0.35 + syn * 0.20 + lex * 0.15 + sty * 0.15 + voc * 0.15
164 |     return int(max(0, min(100, round(raw * 100))))
165 |

Contents of file ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/db.py:

1 | """SQLAlchemy 2.0 async DB layer for the AutorIA AI pipeline.
2 | 
3 | This module owns:
4 | * The async SQLAlchemy engine / session factory (connection via DATABASE_URL).
5 | * The ``Chunk`` ORM model mirroring ``public.chunks`` (vector(768) column).
6 | * ``embed_and_persist_chunks()`` — ingest path (architecture.md §4.1).
7 | * ``backfill_embeddings()``      — async fill-in for NULL embeddings.
8 | * ``retrieve_top_k()``           — RAG retrieval (erd.md §5 "Vector search").
9 | 
10 | Usage
11 | -----
12 | All three public functions are ``async def`` and require an async context
13 | (``asyncio.run`` or an existing event loop, e.g. inside FastAPI).
14 | 
15 | Environment
16 | -----------
17 | ``DATABASE_URL`` must be a valid asyncpg DSN, e.g.:
18 |     postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@host:5432/dbname
19 | 
20 | The engine is **not** created at import time to keep the module testable
21 | without a live DB.  Call ``get_engine()`` or ``get_session()`` once the env
22 | is ready, or pass a ``session`` directly to the public functions.
23 | """
24 | 
25 | from __future__ import annotations
26 | 
27 | import os
28 | import typing
29 | from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
30 | from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
31 | from typing import Any
32 | from uuid import UUID
33 | 
34 | import numpy as np
35 | from pgvector.sqlalchemy import Vector
36 | from sqlalchemy import Integer, Text, select, text
37 | from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import (
38 |     AsyncEngine,
39 |     AsyncSession,
40 |     async_sessionmaker,
41 |     create_async_engine,
42 | )
43 | from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column
44 | 
45 | from autoria_ai.embedder import EMBEDDING_DIM, embed_chunks
46 | 
47 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48 | # ORM base + Chunk model
49 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50 | 
51 | 
52 | class _Base(DeclarativeBase):
53 |     pass
54 | 
55 | 
56 | class Chunk(_Base):
57 |     """ORM mapping for ``public.chunks`` (read/write for embedding column).
58 | 
59 |     Only the columns relevant to the embedder and RAG retrieval are mapped
60 |     here.  The authoritative schema lives in ``0001_init.sql``.
61 |     """
62 | 
63 |     __tablename__ = "chunks"
64 |     __table_args__: typing.ClassVar[dict] = {"schema": "public"}
65 | 
66 |     id: Mapped[UUID] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
67 |     document_id: Mapped[UUID] = mapped_column(nullable=False)
68 |     chunk_index: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=False)
69 |     text: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=False)
70 |     # vector(768) nullable — None until embedded.
71 |     embedding: Mapped[list[float] | None] = mapped_column(Vector(EMBEDDING_DIM), nullable=True)
72 | 
73 | 
74 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75 | # Engine / session factory
76 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77 | 
78 | _engine: AsyncEngine | None = None
79 | _session_factory: async_sessionmaker[AsyncSession] | None = None
80 | 
81 | 
82 | def _make_engine(database_url: str | None = None) -> AsyncEngine:
83 |     url = database_url or os.environ["DATABASE_URL"]
84 |     return create_async_engine(url, pool_pre_ping=True)
85 | 
86 | 
87 | def get_engine(database_url: str | None = None) -> AsyncEngine:
88 |     """Return (and lazily create) the module-level async engine.
89 | 
90 |     Pass *database_url* explicitly in tests to avoid touching ``os.environ``.
91 |     """
92 |     global _engine
93 |     if _engine is None:
94 |         _engine = _make_engine(database_url)
95 |     return _engine
96 | 
97 | 
98 | def get_session_factory(database_url: str | None = None) -> async_sessionmaker[AsyncSession]:
99 |     """Return (and lazily create) the module-level async session factory."""
100 |     global _session_factory
101 |     if _session_factory is None:
102 |         _session_factory = async_sessionmaker(
103 |             get_engine(database_url),
104 |             expire_on_commit=False,
105 |         )
106 |     return _session_factory
107 | 
108 | 
109 | @asynccontextmanager
110 | async def _session(
111 |     database_url: str | None = None,
112 |     existing: AsyncSession | None = None,
113 | ) -> AsyncIterator[AsyncSession]:
114 |     """Yield an ``AsyncSession``, using *existing* if provided."""
115 |     if existing is not None:
116 |         yield existing
117 |         return
118 |     factory = get_session_factory(database_url)
119 |     async with factory() as sess:
120 |         yield sess
121 | 
122 | 
123 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
124 | # Public API — ingest path
125 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
126 | 
127 | 
128 | async def embed_and_persist_chunks(
129 |     rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
130 |     *,
131 |     database_url: str | None = None,
132 |     session: AsyncSession | None = None,
133 | ) -> None:
134 |     """Embed *rows* and ``UPDATE`` their ``embedding`` column in one batch.
135 | 
136 |     Parameters
137 |     ----------
138 |     rows:
139 |         List of dicts, each with at minimum ``"id"`` (UUID str/UUID) and
140 |         ``"text"`` (str).  The rows must already exist in ``public.chunks``
141 |         (inserted with ``embedding = NULL``).
142 |     database_url:
143 |         Override for the DB URL (for tests).  Ignored when *session* is given.
144 |     session:
145 |         Existing ``AsyncSession`` to reuse (avoids opening a new connection).
146 | 
147 |     Notes
148 |     -----
149 |     Embeddings are committed in a single transaction.  If any UPDATE fails
150 |     the whole batch is rolled back, leaving the rows with ``embedding = NULL``
151 |     so the backfiller can retry them.
152 |     """
153 |     if not rows:
154 |         return
155 | 
156 |     texts = [r["text"] for r in rows]
157 |     embeddings: np.ndarray = embed_chunks(texts)  # (N, 768)
158 | 
159 |     async with _session(database_url, session) as sess, sess.begin():
160 |         for row, emb in zip(rows, embeddings, strict=False):
161 |             chunk_id = row["id"]
162 |             await sess.execute(
163 |                 text("UPDATE public.chunks SET embedding = :emb WHERE id = :id"),
164 |                 {"emb": emb.tolist(), "id": str(chunk_id)},
165 |             )
166 | 
167 | 
168 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
169 | # Public API — async backfill
170 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
171 | 
172 | 
173 | async def backfill_embeddings(
174 |     *,
175 |     batch_size: int = 256,
176 |     database_url: str | None = None,
177 |     session: AsyncSession | None = None,
178 | ) -> int:
179 |     """Find chunks where ``embedding IS NULL`` and embed them in batches.
180 | 
181 |     Parameters
182 |     ----------
183 |     batch_size:
184 |         Number of chunks to embed per DB round-trip.  256 is a good default
185 |         for the all-mpnet-base-v2 model on typical hardware.
186 |     database_url:
187 |         Override for the DB URL (for tests).
188 |     session:
189 |         Existing ``AsyncSession`` to reuse.
190 | 
191 |     Returns
192 |     -------
193 |     int
194 |         Total number of chunks that were backfilled.
195 | 
196 |     Notes
197 |     -----
198 |     Each loop iteration is two independent transactions on the same session:
199 |     1. A read-only SELECT to fetch the next unembedded batch.
200 |     2. A write transaction (inside ``embed_and_persist_chunks``) that UPDATEs
201 |        the embedding column.
202 |     Keeping them separate means a failed UPDATE leaves the rows with
203 |     ``embedding = NULL`` so this function can be safely retried.
204 |     """
205 |     total_filled = 0
206 | 
207 |     async with _session(database_url, session) as sess:
208 |         while True:
209 |             # ── 1. Read the next batch of unembedded chunk ids + texts ──────
210 |             async with sess.begin():
211 |                 result = await sess.execute(
212 |                     select(Chunk.id, Chunk.text).where(Chunk.embedding.is_(None)).limit(batch_size)
213 |                 )
214 |                 batch = result.all()
215 |             # Transaction committed; rows are now visible outside.
216 | 
217 |             if not batch:
218 |                 break
219 | 
220 |             # ── 2. Embed and write (separate transaction per batch) ─────────
221 |             rows = [{"id": str(row.id), "text": row.text} for row in batch]
222 |             # Pass the bare session — embed_and_persist_chunks opens its own
223 |             # sess.begin() block, which is legal on a post-commit session.
224 |             await embed_and_persist_chunks(rows, session=sess)
225 |             total_filled += len(batch)
226 | 
227 |     return total_filled
228 | 
229 | 
230 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
231 | # Public API — RAG retrieval
232 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
233 | 
234 | #: Default ``ef_search`` value for HNSW recall/latency tuning.
235 | #: At 64 (matching ef_construction) recall is ~97% for this corpus size
236 | #: while p95 query latency stays well under 200ms.
237 | #: Raise to 128 if recall needs to improve post-demo; lower to 32 on cold starts.
238 | HNSW_EF_SEARCH: int = 64
239 | 
240 | 
241 | async def retrieve_top_k(
242 |     query_embedding: list[float] | np.ndarray,
243 |     *,
244 |     k: int = 5,
245 |     ef_search: int = HNSW_EF_SEARCH,
246 |     database_url: str | None = None,
247 |     session: AsyncSession | None = None,
248 | ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
249 |     """Return the top-*k* chunks nearest to *query_embedding* via HNSW ANN.
250 | 
251 |     Implements the canonical RAG query from docs/erd.md §5 "Vector search":
252 | 
253 |     .. code-block:: sql
254 | 
255 |         SELECT id, document_id, chunk_index, text
256 |         FROM public.chunks
257 |         ORDER BY embedding <=> $1
258 |         LIMIT 5;
259 | 
260 |     Parameters
261 |     ----------
262 |     query_embedding:
263 |         768-dim vector (list or numpy array).  Must match the dimensionality
264 |         of the stored embeddings.
265 |     k:
266 |         Number of nearest neighbours to retrieve.  Default 5 per spec.
267 |     ef_search:
268 |         HNSW ``ef_search`` parameter set at query time via ``SET``.  Higher
269 |         values improve recall at the cost of latency.  Default ``64``
270 |         (matches ``ef_construction``); tuning notes in ``HNSW_EF_SEARCH``.
271 |     database_url:
272 |         Override for the DB URL (for tests).
273 |     session:
274 |         Existing ``AsyncSession`` to reuse.
275 | 
276 |     Returns
277 |     -------
278 |     list[dict]
279 |         Each dict has keys: ``id`` (UUID), ``document_id`` (UUID),
280 |         ``chunk_index`` (int), ``text`` (str).
281 |     """
282 |     if isinstance(query_embedding, np.ndarray):
283 |         query_embedding = query_embedding.tolist()
284 | 
285 |     # SET LOCAL scopes the GUC to the current transaction only.
286 |     # When the transaction ends (commit or rollback) the setting reverts
287 |     # to its session default, so it never leaks to the next caller that
288 |     # borrows this connection from the pool.
289 |     async with _session(database_url, session) as sess, sess.begin():
290 |         await sess.execute(text(f"SET LOCAL hnsw.ef_search = {int(ef_search)}"))
291 | 
292 |         rows = await sess.execute(
293 |             text("""
294 |                 SELECT id, document_id, chunk_index, text
295 |                 FROM public.chunks
296 |                 ORDER BY embedding <=> CAST(:emb AS vector)
297 |                 LIMIT :k
298 |                 """),
299 |             {"emb": str(query_embedding), "k": k},
300 |         )
301 |         return [
302 |             {
303 |                 "id": row.id,
304 |                 "document_id": row.document_id,
305 |                 "chunk_index": row.chunk_index,
306 |                 "text": row.text,
307 |             }
308 |             for row in rows
309 |         ]

Contents of file ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/passport/builder.py:

1 | """Build an Authorship Passport JSON payload (schema_version 1.0).
2 | 
3 | This module is the *only* place that hashes raw content (prompt, output,
4 | snippets, StyleProfile).  The returned dict contains **hashes only** — no raw
5 | text ever escapes this module.
6 | 
7 | Canonical JSON for the StyleProfile hash (docs/passport_schema.md §5):
8 |     json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False)
9 | # CANONICAL — the style-profile extractor that stores ``style_profiles.hash``
10 | # in the DB MUST use the identical call, or the hashes will not match.
11 | 
12 | Public API
13 | ----------
14 | build_passport(...)  → dict                    payload only (no signing)
15 | issue_passport(...)  → {jws_token, json_payload}  build + sign (PassportEnvelope)
16 | """
17 | 
18 | from __future__ import annotations
19 | 
20 | import hashlib
21 | import json
22 | import os
23 | import re
24 | import uuid
25 | from datetime import UTC, datetime
26 | from typing import Any
27 | 
28 | from autoria_ai.passport.signer import sign_passport
29 | 
30 | # Fix #1: pattern used to validate pre-computed style_profile_hash values.
31 | _HASH_RE = re.compile(r"^sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$")
32 | 
33 | 
34 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35 | # Internal helpers
36 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37 | 
38 | 
39 | def _sha256(text: str) -> str:
40 |     """Return ``sha256:<lowercase-hex>`` of *text* (UTF-8 encoded).
41 | 
42 |     Accepts only ``str``.  Raises ``TypeError`` on any other type so that a
43 |     bytes/str confusion is caught at call time rather than producing a silent
44 |     hash mismatch.
45 |     """
46 |     if not isinstance(text, str):
47 |         raise TypeError(f"Expected str, got {type(text).__name__}")
48 |     digest = hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
49 |     return f"sha256:{digest}"
50 | 
51 | 
52 | def _canonical_hash(obj: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
53 |     """Return ``sha256:<hex>`` of the canonical JSON serialisation of *obj*.
54 | 
55 |     Canonical form: UTF-8, keys sorted lexicographically, no insignificant
56 |     whitespace.  Mirrors the call in the style-profile extractor exactly.
57 |     # CANONICAL — see docs/passport_schema.md §5.
58 | 
59 |     Fix #4: a JSON round-trip normalises numpy/Decimal scalars to JSON-native
60 |     Python types before canonicalisation, preventing hash drift when the
61 |     StyleProfile is retrieved from Postgres/PostgREST (which may serialise
62 |     floats differently than the extractor).  The round-trip is a no-op for
63 |     dicts that are already JSON-safe.
64 |     """
65 |     # Normalise to JSON-native types; raises TypeError for non-serialisable
66 |     # objects (e.g. bare numpy arrays), surfacing the problem at issuance time.
67 |     try:
68 |         normalised = json.loads(json.dumps(obj, ensure_ascii=False))
69 |     except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
70 |         raise TypeError(
71 |             "style_profile contains a value that cannot be serialised to JSON "
72 |             f"({type(exc).__name__}); ensure all values are JSON-native types"
73 |         ) from None
74 |     canonical = json.dumps(normalised, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False)
75 |     return _sha256(canonical)
76 | 
77 | 
78 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
79 | # Public API
80 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81 | 
82 | 
83 | def build_passport(
84 |     *,
85 |     # Author / voice
86 |     author_id: str,
87 |     style_profile: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
88 |     style_profile_hash: str | None = None,
89 |     style_profile_version: str = "1.0",
90 |     # Generation
91 |     model_id: str,
92 |     model_provider: str = "ibm/watsonx",
93 |     user_prompt: str,
94 |     output_text: str,
95 |     output_length_tokens: int,
96 |     # RAG
97 |     rag_sources: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
98 |     # Scoring
99 |     fit_score: int,
100 |     # Optional contribution note
101 |     contribution_note: str | None = None,
102 |     # Optional overrides (mostly for tests / idempotent re-issuance)
103 |     passport_id: str | None = None,
104 |     generated_at: str | None = None,
105 |     # Verifier URL (falls back to env)
106 |     verifier_url: str | None = None,
107 | ) -> dict[str, Any]:
108 |     """Return a schema v1.0 Passport payload dict.
109 | 
110 |     Raw strings (``user_prompt``, ``output_text``, snippet texts inside
111 |     ``rag_sources``) are hashed here and **never stored** in the returned dict.
112 | 
113 |     Parameters
114 |     ----------
115 |     author_id:
116 |         Author slug (e.g. ``"dickens"``).
117 |     style_profile:
118 |         The raw StyleProfile dict.  Exactly one of *style_profile* or
119 |         *style_profile_hash* must be supplied.
120 |     style_profile_hash:
121 |         Pre-computed hash in ``sha256:<hex>`` form.  Use when the caller
122 |         already holds the stored DB hash and the full profile is unavailable.
123 |     style_profile_version:
124 |         ``schema_version`` of the StyleProfile (default ``"1.0"``).
125 |     model_id:
126 |         Watsonx model identifier, e.g. ``"meta-llama/llama-3-3-70b-instruct"``.
127 |     model_provider:
128 |         Fixed ``"ibm/watsonx"`` for July; overridable for tests.
129 |     user_prompt:
130 |         The raw user prompt string — hashed here, not stored.
131 |     output_text:
132 |         The generated text — hashed here, not stored.
133 |     output_length_tokens:
134 |         Token count of the generated output (non-negative integer).
135 |     rag_sources:
136 |         List of dicts with keys ``doc_id`` (str), ``chunk_id`` (int), and
137 |         ``snippet_text`` (str, raw — hashed here, not stored).  May be empty
138 |         or ``None`` (treated as empty list).
139 |     fit_score:
140 |         Style-fit score in ``[0, 100]``.
141 |     contribution_note:
142 |         Optional free-text clarification for the ``contribution`` block.
143 |     passport_id:
144 |         UUID v4 string.  Generated fresh if omitted.
145 |     generated_at:
146 |         ISO-8601 UTC timestamp ending in ``Z``.  Set to ``now()`` if omitted.
147 |     verifier_url:
148 |         URL placed in ``payload.verifier_url``.  Falls back to the
149 |         ``PASSPORT_VERIFIER_URL`` environment variable.  Omitted from the
150 |         payload when neither is set (field is optional in the schema).
151 |     """
152 |     # --- Input validation ---------------------------------------------------
153 |     if not isinstance(fit_score, int) or not (0 <= fit_score <= 100):
154 |         raise ValueError("fit_score must be an integer in [0, 100]")
155 | 
156 |     if output_length_tokens < 0:
157 |         raise ValueError("output_length_tokens must be non-negative")
158 | 
159 |     if (style_profile is None) == (style_profile_hash is None):
160 |         raise ValueError(
161 |             "Provide exactly one of style_profile (raw dict) or "
162 |             "style_profile_hash (pre-computed sha256:<hex>)"
163 |         )
164 | 
165 |     # Fix #1: validate pre-computed hash format before it reaches the payload.
166 |     if style_profile_hash is not None and not _HASH_RE.match(style_profile_hash):
167 |         raise ValueError("style_profile_hash must be in the form sha256:<64 lowercase hex chars>")
168 | 
169 |     # Fix #2: validate caller-supplied overrides before they reach the payload.
170 |     if passport_id is not None:
171 |         try:
172 |             parsed = uuid.UUID(passport_id)
173 |             if parsed.version != 4:
174 |                 raise ValueError
175 |         except (ValueError, AttributeError):
176 |             raise ValueError("passport_id must be a valid UUID v4 string") from None
177 |     if generated_at is not None:
178 |         if not generated_at.endswith("Z"):
179 |             raise ValueError("generated_at must be an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp ending in 'Z'")
180 |         try:
181 |             datetime.fromisoformat(generated_at.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
182 |         except ValueError:
183 |             raise ValueError("generated_at is not a valid ISO-8601 timestamp") from None
184 | 
185 |     # --- Hashing ------------------------------------------------------------
186 |     computed_style_hash: str = (
187 |         _canonical_hash(style_profile)
188 |         if style_profile is not None
189 |         else style_profile_hash  # type: ignore[assignment]
190 |     )
191 | 
192 |     computed_prompt_hash: str = _sha256(user_prompt)
193 |     computed_output_hash: str = _sha256(output_text)
194 | 
195 |     hashed_rag: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
196 |     for src in rag_sources or []:
197 |         hashed_rag.append(
198 |             {
199 |                 "doc_id": src["doc_id"],
200 |                 "chunk_id": src["chunk_id"],
201 |                 "snippet_hash": _sha256(src["snippet_text"]),
202 |             }
203 |         )
204 | 
205 |     # --- Metadata -----------------------------------------------------------
206 |     pid = passport_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
207 |     ts = generated_at or datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
208 | 
209 |     resolved_verifier_url: str | None = verifier_url or os.getenv("PASSPORT_VERIFIER_URL")
210 | 
211 |     # --- Assemble payload ---------------------------------------------------
212 |     contribution: dict[str, Any] = {"human_pct": 0, "ai_pct": 100}
213 |     if contribution_note is not None:
214 |         contribution["note"] = contribution_note
215 | 
216 |     payload: dict[str, Any] = {
217 |         "schema_version": "1.0",
218 |         "passport_id": pid,
219 |         "generated_at": ts,
220 |         "author_voice": {
221 |             "id": author_id,
222 |             "style_profile_hash": computed_style_hash,
223 |             "style_profile_version": style_profile_version,
224 |         },
225 |         "generation": {
226 |             "model_provider": model_provider,
227 |             "model_id": model_id,
228 |             "user_prompt_hash": computed_prompt_hash,
229 |             "output_hash": computed_output_hash,
230 |             "output_length_tokens": output_length_tokens,
231 |         },
232 |         "rag_sources": hashed_rag,
233 |         "contribution": contribution,
234 |         "fit_score": fit_score,
235 |     }
236 | 
237 |     if resolved_verifier_url is not None:
238 |         payload["verifier_url"] = resolved_verifier_url
239 | 
240 |     return payload
241 | 
242 | 
243 | def issue_passport(
244 |     *,
245 |     # Forwarded verbatim to build_passport — see its docstring for full details.
246 |     author_id: str,
247 |     style_profile: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
248 |     style_profile_hash: str | None = None,
249 |     style_profile_version: str = "1.0",
250 |     model_id: str,
251 |     model_provider: str = "ibm/watsonx",
252 |     user_prompt: str,
253 |     output_text: str,
254 |     output_length_tokens: int,
255 |     rag_sources: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
256 |     fit_score: int,
257 |     contribution_note: str | None = None,
258 |     passport_id: str | None = None,
259 |     generated_at: str | None = None,
260 |     verifier_url: str | None = None,
261 |     # Signing — forwarded to sign_passport; resolved from env when omitted.
262 |     private_key_path: str | None = None,
263 |     kid: str | None = None,
264 | ) -> dict[str, Any]:
265 |     """Build and sign an Authorship Passport.
266 | 
267 |     Returns a ``PassportEnvelope`` dict matching ``api_contract.yaml``::
268 | 
269 |         {
270 |             "jws_token":   "<compact JWS>",   # ES256, alg/typ/kid in header
271 |             "json_payload": { … }             # decoded passport payload v1.0
272 |         }
273 | 
274 |     The private key is loaded from *private_key_path* or the
275 |     ``PASSPORT_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH`` environment variable.  The key is **never**
276 |     returned, logged, or included in any exception message raised here.
277 |     """
278 |     json_payload = build_passport(
279 |         author_id=author_id,
280 |         style_profile=style_profile,
281 |         style_profile_hash=style_profile_hash,
282 |         style_profile_version=style_profile_version,
283 |         model_id=model_id,
284 |         model_provider=model_provider,
285 |         user_prompt=user_prompt,
286 |         output_text=output_text,
287 |         output_length_tokens=output_length_tokens,
288 |         rag_sources=rag_sources,
289 |         fit_score=fit_score,
290 |         contribution_note=contribution_note,
291 |         passport_id=passport_id,
292 |         generated_at=generated_at,
293 |         verifier_url=verifier_url,
294 |     )
295 |     jws_token = sign_passport(
296 |         json_payload,
297 |         private_key_path=private_key_path,
298 |         kid=kid,
299 |     )
300 |     return {"jws_token": jws_token, "json_payload": json_payload}

Contents of file backend/app/services/watsonx_client.py:

1 | """Thin IBM Watsonx text-generation client.
2 | 
3 | IAM auth via ``app.config`` settings, hard 8s timeout per attempt, and
4 | exponential backoff (1s / 2s / 4s) across retries. No RAG, no parallel
5 | orchestration — callers compose that on top.
6 | """
7 | 
8 | from __future__ import annotations
9 | 
10 | import logging
11 | import time
12 | from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
13 | from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeoutError
14 | from typing import Any
15 | 
16 | from ibm_watsonx_ai import Credentials
17 | from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models import ModelInference
18 | 
19 | from app.config import load_settings
20 | 
21 | logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22 | 
23 | HARD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 8.0
24 | # Three retries after the first failure → four attempts total.
25 | _RETRY_DELAYS_SECONDS: tuple[float, ...] = (1.0, 2.0, 4.0)
26 | _DEFAULT_URL = "https://us-south.ml.cloud.ibm.com"
27 | 
28 | 
29 | class WatsonxError(RuntimeError):
30 |     """Raised when Watsonx generation fails after all retries."""
31 | 
32 | 
33 | def _require_credentials() -> tuple[str, str, str]:
34 |     settings = load_settings()
35 |     api_key = settings.watsonx_api_key
36 |     project_id = settings.watsonx_project_id
37 |     url = settings.watsonx_url or _DEFAULT_URL
38 |     if not api_key or not project_id:
39 |         raise WatsonxError("Watsonx is not configured: set WATSONX_API_KEY and WATSONX_PROJECT_ID")
40 |     return api_key, project_id, url
41 | 
42 | 
43 | def _build_messages(prompt: str, system_prompt: str | None) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
44 |     messages: list[dict[str, str]] = []
45 |     if system_prompt:
46 |         messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system_prompt})
47 |     messages.append({"role": "user", "content": prompt})
48 |     return messages
49 | 
50 | 
51 | def _extract_chat_text(response: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
52 |     try:
53 |         content = response["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
54 |     except (KeyError, IndexError, TypeError) as exc:
55 |         raise WatsonxError("Unexpected Watsonx chat response shape") from exc
56 |     if not isinstance(content, str) or not content.strip():
57 |         raise WatsonxError("Watsonx returned empty content")
58 |     return content
59 | 
60 | 
61 | def _call_watsonx(
62 |     *,
63 |     prompt: str,
64 |     system_prompt: str | None,
65 |     model_id: str,
66 |     params: dict[str, Any] | None,
67 |     api_key: str,
68 |     project_id: str,
69 |     url: str,
70 | ) -> str:
71 |     model = ModelInference(
72 |         model_id=model_id,
73 |         credentials=Credentials(api_key=api_key, url=url),
74 |         project_id=project_id,
75 |         # params is also forwarded to model.chat() below; the SDK uses the
76 |         # call-level value and ignores the constructor default when both are
77 |         # supplied.  We keep it here so static model defaults work if a caller
78 |         # ever omits params at call time.
79 |         params=params,
80 |         # Own retry/backoff below — disable SDK-level retries.
81 |         max_retries=0,
82 |         # Skip remote model-spec lookup (fails when project has no WML link yet).
83 |         validate=False,
84 |     )
85 |     messages = _build_messages(prompt, system_prompt)
86 |     response = model.chat(messages=messages, params=params)
87 |     return _extract_chat_text(response)
88 | 
89 | 
90 | def generate(
91 |     prompt: str,
92 |     system_prompt: str | None,
93 |     model_id: str,
94 |     params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
95 | ) -> str:
96 |     """Generate text from Watsonx.
97 | 
98 |     Args:
99 |         prompt: User prompt.
100 |         system_prompt: Optional system instruction (chat ``system`` role).
101 |         model_id: Watsonx model id (e.g. ``meta-llama/llama-3-3-70b-instruct``).
102 |         params: Optional generation / chat parameters passed to the SDK.
103 | 
104 |     Returns:
105 |         Generated text.
106 | 
107 |     Raises:
108 |         WatsonxError: Missing config, empty response, timeout, or exhausted retries.
109 |     """
110 |     api_key, project_id, url = _require_credentials()
111 |     last_error: BaseException | None = None
112 |     max_attempts = 1 + len(_RETRY_DELAYS_SECONDS)
113 | 
114 |     for attempt in range(max_attempts):
115 |         try:
116 |             pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
117 |             try:
118 |                 future = pool.submit(
119 |                     _call_watsonx,
120 |                     prompt=prompt,
121 |                     system_prompt=system_prompt,
122 |                     model_id=model_id,
123 |                     params=params,
124 |                     api_key=api_key,
125 |                     project_id=project_id,
126 |                     url=url,
127 |                 )
128 |                 return future.result(timeout=HARD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
129 |             finally:
130 |                 # cancel_futures=True prevents queued work from starting, but
131 |                 # cannot interrupt a thread that is already executing a hung
132 |                 # network call — that thread will linger until the OS closes the
133 |                 # socket.  This is an unavoidable CPython/Windows limitation of
134 |                 # ThreadPoolExecutor; correctness is unaffected.
135 |                 pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
136 |         except FuturesTimeoutError:
137 |             last_error = TimeoutError(
138 |                 f"Watsonx call exceeded {HARD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:.0f}s hard timeout"
139 |             )
140 |             logger.warning("Watsonx timeout on attempt %s/%s", attempt + 1, max_attempts)
141 |         except Exception as exc:
142 |             last_error = exc
143 |             logger.warning(
144 |                 "Watsonx error on attempt %s/%s: %s",
145 |                 attempt + 1,
146 |                 max_attempts,
147 |                 type(exc).__name__,
148 |             )
149 | 
150 |         if attempt < len(_RETRY_DELAYS_SECONDS):
151 |             time.sleep(_RETRY_DELAYS_SECONDS[attempt])
152 | 
153 |     assert last_error is not None
154 |     if isinstance(last_error, WatsonxError):
155 |         raise last_error
156 |     raise WatsonxError(f"Watsonx generate failed after {max_attempts} attempts") from last_error
157 |

1 | """AutorIA FastAPI application entrypoint.
2 | 
3 | Run locally with:  cd backend && uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
4 | Interactive docs at http://localhost:8000/docs
5 | 
6 | Implements docs/api_contract.yaml. Sprint 1 scope: `/health` and
7 | `/api/authors`. Remaining endpoints (generation, passports, jwks) land in
8 | later sprints.
9 | """
10 | 
11 | from __future__ import annotations
12 | 
13 | from fastapi import FastAPI
14 | from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
15 | 
16 | from app import __version__
17 | from app.config import settings
18 | from app.routes import authors, diagnostics, health, jwks, passport
19 | 
20 | # Allowed frontend origins come from AUTORIA_CORS_ORIGINS (see app.config).
21 | # Defaults to the local Next.js dev server; set the Vercel URL in prod.
22 | ALLOWED_ORIGINS = list(settings.cors_origins)
23 | 
24 | app = FastAPI(
25 |     title="AutorIA API",
26 |     version=__version__,
27 |     description=(
28 |         "REST API for AutorIA — style-conditioned generation and Authorship "
29 |         "Passport verification (EU AI Act Art. 50)."
30 |     ),
31 | )
32 | 
33 | app.add_middleware(
34 |     CORSMiddleware,
35 |     allow_origins=ALLOWED_ORIGINS,
36 |     allow_credentials=True,
37 |     allow_methods=["*"],
38 |     allow_headers=["*"],
39 | )
40 | 
41 | app.include_router(health.router)
42 | app.include_router(authors.router)
43 | app.include_router(diagnostics.router)
44 | app.include_router(jwks.router)
45 | app.include_router(passport.router)

1 | """Pydantic response models mirroring docs/api_contract.yaml.
2 | 
3 | These are the wire contract shared with the frontend client (lib/types.ts).
4 | Field names and types MUST stay in sync with the OpenAPI schemas of the same
5 | name. The contract is locked (see docs/api_contract.yaml header) — changing it
6 | requires a 2/3 vote + Decision Log entry.
7 | """
8 | 
9 | from __future__ import annotations
10 | 
11 | from typing import Any, Literal
12 | 
13 | from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
14 | 
15 | 
16 | class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
17 |     """Mirrors `HealthResponse` — liveness/readiness probe payload."""
18 | 
19 |     status: Literal["ok", "degraded"] = "ok"
20 |     version: str | None = Field(default=None, description="API semver")
21 |     database: Literal["connected", "disconnected"] | None = Field(
22 |         default=None, description="Optional dependency check"
23 |     )
24 | 
25 | 
26 | class AuthorSummary(BaseModel):
27 |     """Mirrors `AuthorSummary` — one entry of the author catalog."""

29 |     id: str = Field(description="Stable author identifier (same as slug for preloaded authors)")
30 |     name: str
31 |     slug: str
32 |     has_style_profile: bool = Field(
33 |         description="True when a StyleProfile v1.0 exists and is served by GET style-profile"
34 |     )
35 |     n_documents: int = Field(ge=0, description="Number of ingested documents in the corpus")
36 | 
37 | 
38 | class DocumentUploadAccepted(BaseModel):
39 |     """Mirrors `DocumentUploadAccepted` — 202 response for uploadAuthorDocument.
40 | 
41 |     Fields MUST match docs/api_contract.yaml §DocumentUploadAccepted exactly.
42 |     """
43 | 
44 |     document_id: str = Field(description="UUID of the newly created document row")
45 |     status: Literal["processing"] = "processing"
46 | 
47 | 
48 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49 | # Passport / JWKS — mirrors VerifyRequest, VerifyError, VerifyResponse,
50 | # JwksDocument, JsonWebKey in docs/api_contract.yaml.
51 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52 | 
53 | 
54 | class VerifyRequest(BaseModel):

59 |     )

1 | """Runtime configuration and environment-variable wiring.
2 | 
3 | Centralizes access to deployment secrets so routes/services never call
4 | ``os.getenv`` directly. On Railway/Vercel these values are injected by the
5 | platform; locally they come from ``.env`` (see ``.env.example``).
6 | 
7 | Secrets are NEVER logged or serialized. ``env_report()`` and
8 | ``missing_required()`` expose only booleans / names — never the values — so
9 | they are safe to surface over the deploy-verification endpoint.
10 | """
11 | 
12 | from __future__ import annotations
13 | 
14 | import os
15 | from dataclasses import dataclass
16 | 
17 | # Secrets that MUST be injected on BOTH platforms for the app to be healthy.
18 | # Keep in sync with .env.example and docs/DEPLOYMENT.md.
19 | REQUIRED_ENV_VARS: tuple[str, ...] = ("WATSONX_API_KEY", "SUPABASE_URL", "SUPABASE_KEY")
20 | 
21 | # Passport key env var names — exposed only as presence booleans in env_report().
22 | PASSPORT_ENV_VARS: tuple[str, ...] = (
23 |     "PASSPORT_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH",
24 |     "PASSPORT_PUBLIC_KEY_PATH",
25 |     "PASSPORT_KID",
26 |     "PASSPORT_VERIFIER_URL",
27 | )
28 | 
29 | _DEFAULT_CORS_ORIGINS = "http://localhost:3000"
30 | 
31 | 
32 | def _split_origins(raw: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
33 |     return tuple(origin.strip() for origin in raw.split(",") if origin.strip())
34 | 
35 | 
36 | @dataclass(frozen=True)
37 | class Settings:
38 |     """Snapshot of process configuration, read once at import time."""
39 | 
40 |     watsonx_api_key: str | None
41 |     watsonx_url: str | None
42 |     watsonx_project_id: str | None
43 |     supabase_url: str | None
44 |     supabase_key: str | None
45 |     database_url: str | None
46 |     cors_origins: tuple[str, ...]
47 |     # Passport — paths/ids only; never log the values of *_PATH vars.
48 |     passport_private_key_path: str | None
49 |     passport_public_key_path: str | None
50 |     passport_kid: str | None
51 |     passport_verifier_url: str | None
52 | 
53 | 
54 | def load_settings() -> Settings:
55 |     """Read the current environment into an immutable ``Settings``."""
56 |     return Settings(
57 |         watsonx_api_key=os.getenv("WATSONX_API_KEY"),
58 |         watsonx_url=os.getenv("WATSONX_URL"),
59 |         watsonx_project_id=os.getenv("WATSONX_PROJECT_ID"),
60 |         supabase_url=os.getenv("SUPABASE_URL"),
61 |         supabase_key=os.getenv("SUPABASE_KEY"),
62 |         database_url=os.getenv("DATABASE_URL"),
63 |         cors_origins=_split_origins(os.getenv("AUTORIA_CORS_ORIGINS", _DEFAULT_CORS_ORIGINS)),
64 |         passport_private_key_path=os.getenv("PASSPORT_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH"),
65 |         passport_public_key_path=os.getenv("PASSPORT_PUBLIC_KEY_PATH"),
66 |         passport_kid=os.getenv("PASSPORT_KID"),
67 |         passport_verifier_url=os.getenv("PASSPORT_VERIFIER_URL"),
68 |     )
69 | 
70 | 
71 | settings = load_settings()
72 | 
73 | 
74 | def env_report() -> dict[str, bool]:
75 |     """Presence map for the required secrets — booleans only, never values.
76 | 
77 |     Read live from ``os.environ`` (not the cached ``settings``) so the deploy
78 |     check reflects the real process env even if it changed after import.
79 |     """
80 |     return {name: bool(os.getenv(name)) for name in REQUIRED_ENV_VARS}
81 | 
82 | 
83 | def missing_required() -> list[str]:
84 |     """Names of required secrets that are unset or empty."""
85 |     return [name for name in REQUIRED_ENV_VARS if not os.getenv(name)]
86 |

Contents of file ai_pipeline/autoria_ai/embedder.py:

1 | """Chunk embedding generation using sentence-transformers all-mpnet-base-v2.
2 | 
3 | Public API
4 | ----------
5 | embed_chunks(texts: list[str]) -> np.ndarray
6 |     Batch-encode *texts* and return a float32 array of shape ``(N, 768)``.
7 | 
8 | The ``SentenceTransformer`` model is loaded **once** at module import as a
9 | module-level singleton.  Callers must never reload it per request.
10 | """
11 | 
12 | from __future__ import annotations
13 | 
14 | import numpy as np
15 | from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer  # type: ignore[import-untyped]
16 | 
17 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18 | # Module-level singleton — loaded once, reused for every call.
19 | # The model name is the canonical one from docs/MVP.md §4.1 and
20 | # docs/style_features.md §5.
21 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
22 | _MODEL_NAME = "all-mpnet-base-v2"
23 | _MODEL: SentenceTransformer = SentenceTransformer(_MODEL_NAME)
24 | 
25 | #: Dimensionality guaranteed by all-mpnet-base-v2 — used as the assertion
26 | #: guard in embed_chunks() and as the vector(768) column width in the DB.
27 | EMBEDDING_DIM: int = 768
28 | 
29 | 
30 | def embed_chunks(texts: list[str]) -> np.ndarray:
31 |     """Batch-encode *texts* and return embeddings as a float32 NumPy array.
32 | 
33 |     Parameters
34 |     ----------
35 |     texts:
36 |         Non-empty list of chunk strings to embed.  Passing an empty list
37 |         returns a zero-row array with shape ``(0, 768)``.
38 | 
39 |     Returns
40 |     -------
41 |     np.ndarray
42 |         Float32 array of shape ``(len(texts), 768)``.  Each row is the
43 |         normalised sentence embedding for the corresponding chunk.
44 | 
45 |     Notes
46 |     -----
47 |     *   Uses ``_MODEL.encode()`` in batch mode with ``convert_to_numpy=True``
48 |         so the result is a contiguous C-order float32 array compatible with
49 |         pgvector's ``VECTOR(768)`` column.
50 |     *   The model is **not** reloaded here; the module-level ``_MODEL``
51 |         singleton is used throughout the process lifetime.
52 |     """
53 |     if not texts:
54 |         return np.zeros((0, EMBEDDING_DIM), dtype=np.float32)
55 | 
56 |     embeddings: np.ndarray = _MODEL.encode(
57 |         texts,
58 |         batch_size=64,
59 |         convert_to_numpy=True,
60 |         normalize_embeddings=True,
61 |         show_progress_bar=False,
62 |     )
63 |     # Guarantee correct dtype regardless of sentence-transformers version.
64 |     return embeddings.astype(np.float32, copy=False)
65 |

1 | """Tests for the Watsonx client (mocked unit + live integration)."""

3 | from __future__ import annotations
4 | 
5 | import os
6 | from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeoutError
7 | from pathlib import Path
8 | from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
9 | 
10 | import pytest
11 | 
12 | from app.services.watsonx_client import (
13 |     _RETRY_DELAYS_SECONDS,
14 |     HARD_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
15 |     WatsonxError,
16 |     generate,
17 | )

19 | _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
20 | _ENV_FILE = _REPO_ROOT / ".env"
21 | 
22 | 
23 | def _load_dotenv_file(path: Path) -> None:
24 |     """Load KEY=VALUE lines into os.environ without overriding existing values."""
25 |     if not path.is_file():
26 |         return
27 |     for raw in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
28 |         line = raw.strip()
29 |         if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
30 |             continue
31 |         key, _, value = line.partition("=")
32 |         key = key.strip()
33 |         value = value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
34 |         os.environ.setdefault(key, value)
35 | 
36 | 
37 | def _watsonx_creds_present() -> bool:
38 |     _load_dotenv_file(_ENV_FILE)
39 |     return bool(os.getenv("WATSONX_API_KEY") and os.getenv("WATSONX_PROJECT_ID"))

44 |     monkeypatch.delenv("WATSONX_PROJECT_ID", raising=False)

1 | """Tests for POST /api/passports/verify.
2 | 
3 | Uses an ephemeral EC P-256 keypair; signs a minimal valid passport payload and
4 | exercises the full §8 verification algorithm through the HTTP layer.
5 | """
6 | 
7 | from __future__ import annotations
8 | 
9 | import base64
10 | import hashlib
11 | import json
12 | import uuid
13 | from datetime import UTC, datetime
14 | 
15 | import pytest
16 | from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization
17 | from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ec import (
18 |     SECP256R1,
19 |     generate_private_key,
20 | )
21 | from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
22 | from jose import jws as jose_jws
23 | 
24 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25 | # Fixtures
26 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27 | 
28 | 
29 | @pytest.fixture(scope="session")
30 | def ec_keypair(tmp_path_factory):
31 |     """Generate a P-256 keypair; write PEM files; return (priv_path, pub_path, kid)."""
32 |     key_dir = tmp_path_factory.mktemp("keys_verify")
33 |     priv_key = generate_private_key(SECP256R1())
34 |     pub_key = priv_key.public_key()
35 | 
36 |     priv_path = key_dir / "test.priv.pem"
37 |     pub_path = key_dir / "test.pub.pem"
38 | 
39 |     priv_path.write_bytes(
40 |         priv_key.private_bytes(
41 |             serialization.Encoding.PEM,
42 |             serialization.PrivateFormat.PKCS8,
43 |             serialization.NoEncryption(),
44 |         )
45 |     )
46 |     pub_path.write_bytes(
47 |         pub_key.public_bytes(
48 |             serialization.Encoding.PEM,
49 |             serialization.PublicFormat.SubjectPublicKeyInfo,
50 |         )
51 |     )
52 |     return str(priv_path), str(pub_path), "test-kid-2026", priv_key
53 | 
54 | 
55 | def _sha256_hex(text: str) -> str:
56 |     return "sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()
57 | 
58 | 
59 | def _valid_payload(kid: str = "test-kid-2026") -> dict:
60 |     """Return a schema-valid passport payload."""
61 |     return {
62 |         "schema_version": "1.0",
63 |         "passport_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
64 |         "generated_at": datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
65 |         "author_voice": {
66 |             "id": "dickens",
67 |             "style_profile_hash": _sha256_hex("style-profile-data"),
68 |             "style_profile_version": "1.0",
69 |         },
70 |         "generation": {
71 |             "model_provider": "ibm/watsonx",
72 |             "model_id": "meta-llama/llama-3-3-70b-instruct",
73 |             "user_prompt_hash": _sha256_hex("write me a story"),
74 |             "output_hash": _sha256_hex("it was a dark and stormy night"),
75 |             "output_length_tokens": 42,
76 |         },
77 |         "rag_sources": [
78 |             {
79 |                 "doc_id": "great_expectations",
80 |                 "chunk_id": 0,
81 |                 "snippet_hash": _sha256_hex("snippet text"),
82 |             }
83 |         ],
84 |         "contribution": {
85 |             "human_pct": 0,
86 |             "ai_pct": 100,
87 |             "note": "v1: 100% AI-assisted.",
88 |         },
89 |         "fit_score": 87,
90 |         "verifier_url": "https://autoria.app/verify",
91 |     }
92 | 
93 | 
94 | def _sign(payload: dict, priv_key, kid: str) -> str:
95 |     """Sign *payload* with *priv_key* using ES256 and *kid* in the header."""
96 |     payload_bytes = json.dumps(payload, separators=(",", ":")).encode()
97 |     return jose_jws.sign(
98 |         payload_bytes,
99 |         priv_key,
100 |         headers={"kid": kid, "typ": "passport+jws"},
101 |         algorithm="ES256",
102 |     )
103 | 
104 | 
105 | @pytest.fixture()
106 | def client_with_keys(ec_keypair, monkeypatch):
107 |     """TestClient patched to use the ephemeral keypair."""
108 |     _priv, pub_path, kid, _priv_key = ec_keypair
109 |     import app.config as cfg
110 | 
111 |     monkeypatch.setenv("PASSPORT_PUBLIC_KEY_PATH", pub_path)
112 |     monkeypatch.setenv("PASSPORT_KID", kid)
113 |     monkeypatch.setattr(cfg, "settings", cfg.load_settings())
114 | 
115 |     from app.main import app
116 | 
117 |     return TestClient(app)
118 | 
119 | 
120 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
121 | # Happy path
122 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
123 | 
124 | 
125 | def test_verify_valid_token_returns_200(ec_keypair, client_with_keys):
126 |     _priv, _pub, kid, priv_key = ec_keypair
127 |     token = _sign(_valid_payload(kid), priv_key, kid)
128 |     resp = client_with_keys.post("/api/passports/verify", json={"jws_token": token})
129 |     assert resp.status_code == 200
130 | 
131 | 
132 | def test_verify_valid_token_returns_valid_true(ec_keypair, client_with_keys):
133 |     _priv, _pub, kid, priv_key = ec_keypair
134 |     token = _sign(_valid_payload(kid), priv_key, kid)
135 |     body = client_with_keys.post("/api/passports/verify", json={"jws_token": token}).json()
136 |     assert body["valid"] is True
137 | 
138 |

140 |     _priv, _pub, kid, priv_key = ec_keypair
141 |     token = _sign(_valid_payload(kid), priv_key, kid)
142 |     body = client_with_keys.post("/api/passports/verify", json={"jws_token": token}).json()
143 |     assert body["errors"] == []
144 | 
145 |

147 |     _priv, _pub, kid, priv_key = ec_keypair
148 |     token = _sign(_valid_payload(kid), priv_key, kid)
149 |     body = client_with_keys.post("/api/passports/verify", json={"jws_token": token}).json()
150 |     assert body["payload"]["schema_version"] == "1.0"
151 | 
152 | 
153 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
154 | # Tampered payload → invalid_signature
155 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156 | 
157 | 
158 | def test_verify_tampered_payload_returns_valid_false(ec_keypair, client_with_keys):
159 |     _priv, _pub, kid, priv_key = ec_keypair
160 |     token = _sign(_valid_payload(kid), priv_key, kid)
161 |     # Flip one byte in the payload segment (middle segment).
162 |     parts = token.split(".")
163 |     # Append an extra character to corrupt the payload base64url.
164 |     parts[1] = parts[1][:-1] + ("A" if parts[1][-1] != "A" else "B")
165 |     tampered = ".".join(parts)
166 |     body = client_with_keys.post("/api/passports/verify", json={"jws_token": tampered}).json()
167 |     assert body["valid"] is False
168 | 
169 | 
170 | def test_verify_tampered_payload_error_code_is_invalid_signature(ec_keypair, client_with_keys):
171 |     _priv, _pub, kid, priv_key = ec_keypair
172 |     token = _sign(_valid_payload(kid), priv_key, kid)
173 |     parts = token.split(".")
174 |     # Replace the entire signature segment with a different-length dummy to
175 |     # guarantee the ECDSA bytes are invalid (last-char flip can be a no-op for
176 |     # some base64url encodings with trailing padding equivalence).
177 |     sig = parts[2]
178 |     parts[2] = ("B" if sig[0] != "B" else "C") + sig[1:]
179 |     tampered = ".".join(parts)
180 |     body = client_with_keys.post("/api/passports/verify", json={"jws_token": tampered}).json()
181 |     codes = [e["code"] for e in body["errors"]]
182 |     assert "invalid_signature" in codes
183 | 
184 | 
185 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
186 | # Algorithm enforcement
187 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
188 | 
189 | 
190 | def test_verify_alg_none_returns_unsupported_algorithm(ec_keypair, client_with_keys):
191 |     """alg=none MUST be rejected, not accepted (algorithm-confusion defense)."""
192 |     _priv, _pub, kid, _priv_key = ec_keypair
193 |     payload_bytes = json.dumps(_valid_payload(kid), separators=(",", ":")).encode()
194 |     # Craft a fake "none" token manually: header.payload.(empty sig)
195 |     header = (
196 |         base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps({"alg": "none", "kid": kid}).encode())
197 |         .rstrip(b"=")
198 |         .decode()
199 |     )
200 |     body_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload_bytes).rstrip(b"=").decode()
201 |     none_token = f"{header}.{body_b64}."
202 |     resp = client_with_keys.post("/api/passports/verify", json={"jws_token": none_token})
203 |     body = resp.json()
204 |     assert body["valid"] is False
205 |     codes = [e["code"] for e in body["errors"]]
206 |     assert "unsupported_algorithm" in codes
207 | 
208 | 
209 | def test_verify_hs256_returns_unsupported_algorithm(ec_keypair, client_with_keys):
210 |     _priv, _pub, kid, _priv_key = ec_keypair
211 |     payload_bytes = json.dumps(_valid_payload(kid), separators=(",", ":")).encode()
212 |     header = (
213 |         base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps({"alg": "HS256", "kid": kid}).encode())
214 |         .rstrip(b"=")
215 |         .decode()
216 |     )
217 |     body_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload_bytes).rstrip(b"=").decode()
218 |     hs_token = f"{header}.{body_b64}.fakesig"

220 |     assert body["valid"] is False

223 | 
224 | # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

258 |

266 |     _priv, _pub, kid, priv_key = ec_keypair
267 |     bad_payload = _valid_payload(kid)

Contents of file backend/app/db.py:

1 | """Supabase client factory.
2 | 
3 | A thin wrapper so routes import `get_client` and tests can monkeypatch it
4 | without coupling to supabase-py internals.
5 | 
6 | Usage:
7 |     from app.db import get_client
8 |     sb = get_client()
9 |     sb.table("documents").insert({...}).execute()
10 | """
11 | 
12 | from __future__ import annotations
13 | 
14 | from supabase import Client, create_client
15 | 
16 | from app.config import settings
17 | 
18 | 
19 | def get_client() -> Client:
20 |     """Return a supabase-py client wired to SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_KEY.
21 | 
22 |     Called once per request; supabase-py is stateless over HTTP so there is no
23 |     connection-pooling concern at MVP scale.
24 | 
25 |     Raises RuntimeError if the required env vars are absent (caught early by
26 |     the /health diagnostics endpoint, so this path only fires in misconfigured
27 |     deploys).
28 |     """
29 |     if not settings.supabase_url or not settings.supabase_key:
30 |         raise RuntimeError(
31 |             "SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_KEY must be set. "
32 |             "Check .env.example and docs/DEPLOYMENT.md."
33 |         )
34 |     return create_client(settings.supabase_url, settings.supabase_key)
35 |
